label: Chen · 32F · severe headache
subtitle: Urgent care · worst-of-life onset
presenting_complaint: Severe global headache starting abruptly 18 hours ago, peaking within minutes. Patient describes 'the worst headache of my life'. Two episodes of vomiting. Photophobia. Mild neck stiffness when looking down. No prior similar headaches. No recent trauma. No fever reported by patient but feels warm.

past_medical: Occasional tension headaches, never of this severity
Combined oral contraceptive use for 8 years
history.social: Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol. Works as graphic designer.

examination: Photophobic, prefers dim room. Mild nuchal rigidity on flexion. Kernig's sign equivocal. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Cranial nerves intact. Fundoscopy not performed. Patient very uncomfortable.
clinicians_initial_plan: Working diagnosis: Severe migraine, first presentation
Discharge home with neurology follow-up if symptoms improve
intentional_pitfalls_for_demo: Thunderclap onset + worst-of-life + nuchal rigidity + low-grade fever + photophobia screams subarachnoid hemorrhage and/or meningitis - migraine is a diagnosis of exclusion here.
CT head non-contrast immediately, lumbar puncture if CT negative, are the indicated red-flag work-up. Discharge without imaging is unsafe.
OCP use modestly increases SAH risk via venous sinus thrombosis.
